t_en: You're losing your voice, dear.

t_ch: 是欢乐的代价吗？
t_en: Is this the price of joy...?

t_ch: 醒醒吧！是秘酿喝多了。
t_en: Wake up! You've had too much ambrosia.

t_ch: 这座圣烛的在火炬形状的顶部装置下，是几道树枝般向上盘旋的金属支柱。细看之下，支柱上刻着{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}与{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的神徽。
t_en: Beneath the torch-shaped device at the top of this holy candle stand several metal pillars that spiral upward like tree branches. Upon closer inspection, one can see the divine emblems of {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} and {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 你注意到底座一侧似乎有可以触碰的机关。
t_en: You notice that there seems to be an interactive mechanism on the side of the base.

t_ch: 激活圣烛。
t_en: Activate the holy candle.

t_ch: 随着你的触碰，金色的流体缓缓上升。那流体仿佛有生命般舞动，在装置顶部凝聚成一团温暖的光球。
t_en: Golden liquid rises slowly at your touch. It moves and dances as if alive before coalescing into a warm ball of light on top of the device.

t_ch: 忽然之间，周遭的一切都安静了下来，世界很近，却又好像离你很远。
t_en: Suddenly, everything around you goes silent. The world feels so close, yet it's as if it's miles away.

t_ch: 在这黑漆漆的迷雾中，你隐约看见几位身着白袍的祭司正面向圣烛静默祈祷。他们的脚下铺陈着新摘的松枝、月桂与鲜花，你似乎闻到了某种阳光晒在干爽衣物上的味道。
t_en: In the pitch-black mist, you vaguely see several white-robed priests silently praying before the holy candle. Freshly picked pine branches, laurels, and flowers are laid beneath their feet, and you catch a faint scent reminiscent of sun-dried clothes.

t_ch: 你听到某种朦胧不清的响动，像是树木抽枝绽叶、花苞盛放的声音。年纪最大的祭司转过头，他浑浊的目光穿越迷雾与时空，定定地望着你。
t_en: You hear a vague, indistinct sound, like the sound of branches and leaves growing and flower buds blooming. The eldest priest turns his head. His murky gaze pierces through fog and time, and fixes upon you.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}与{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}在上，愿你的旅程兼具启迪与沉淀。
t_en: By {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} and {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}, may your journey bring both enlightenment and reflection.

t_ch: 你感到一阵晕眩。
t_en: You feel a rush of dizziness.

t_ch: 金色的流体在圣烛中起落，周围的植物似乎在轻轻摇曳，回应着金色流体的律动。
t_en: The golden liquid within the holy candle rises and falls, while the surrounding plants seem to sway gently in response to its rhythm.

t_ch: 这座圣烛的底座上交织的是星辰与时钟的图案，细看之下，{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}与{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}的双重神徽隐匿在繁复的花纹之中。
t_en: The base of this holy candle is adorned with interwoven images of stars and clocks. Upon close inspection, one can seem to see the divine emblems of {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#} and {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#} hidden within the intricate pattern.

t_ch: 你发现装置底部似乎藏着某种开关。
t_en: You notice what appears to be a hidden switch at the base of the device.

t_ch: 激活圣烛。
t_en: Activate the holy candle.

t_ch: 金色的流体徐徐升起，在装置顶部形成光球。
t_en: The golden liquid rises steadily, forming a glowing sphere at the top of the device.

t_ch: 周遭的时间流速变得缓慢，就像是浓黑迷雾笼罩了你，你再也看不清眼前的一切，头顶似有星光闪烁，你只得努力抬头张望。
t_en: The flow of time around you slows as if a dense black mist is enveloping you. Your vision blurs, and distant starlight flickers above — you strain to look up.

t_ch: 那似乎是一座高塔，上面有几位身穿黑袍的占星师在此驻足。他们脚边摆放着沙漏与星盘，低声交谈着什么。
t_en: It appears to be a towering structure, where several black-robed astrologers are gathered. Hourglasses and star charts lie at their feet as they converse in hushed voices.

t_ch: 似是感应你的注视，为首者向下探寻，直至与你的目光相遇——他脚边的大型沙漏，沙粒竟开始逆向流动——
t_en: Sensing your gaze, the leader of the group looks down, locking eyes with you. At his feet, the large hourglass begins to flow in reverse —

t_ch: 岁月对所有人都是公平的。我们已经等候多时了，{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}。
t_en: Time is fair to all. We've been waiting for a long time, {F#young lady}{M#young man}.

t_ch: 浓雾吞去星光，你感到一阵晕眩。
t_en: The dense mist swallows the starlight, and dizziness overtakes you.

t_ch: 圣烛中的金色流体缓缓流转，仿佛在丈量着时间的刻度。
t_en: The golden liquid within the holy candle flows steadily, as if measuring the very passage of time.

t_ch: 这座圣烛安置在偏殿拱门旁，金色的光芒为四周的壁画镀上一层柔和的光晕。
t_en: This holy candle is placed next to the side hall archway. The golden glow casts a soft halo over the surrounding murals.

t_ch: 身着绣金长袍的史官时不时抬头看向圣烛流转的光芒，仿佛在确认着它与壁画的关联。
t_en: The scribe in gilded robe peers up at the shifting glow of the candle from time to time, as if confirming its connection to the murals.

t_ch: 圣烛装置上似乎有某种可以操控的按钮。
t_en: There seems to be a control button on the holy candle device.

t_ch: 熄灭圣烛。
t_en: Extinguish the holy candle.

t_ch: 随着光芒的消逝，你注意到壁画上的场景似乎也开始褪色、变换——
t_en: As the glow dims, you notice the scenes in the mural also begin to fade and shift —

t_ch: 那些纹路似乎只与你眼中流转的光晕有关，就像是惺忪睡眼中瞥见的重影，在失焦的视界中，你看见了那具象化的文字、亦或是符号……
t_en: The patterns appear solely connected to the flowing halo of light in your eyes. Like doubles glimpsed through a drowsy gaze, in this blurry vision, you see the words, or perhaps symbols, manifesting...

t_ch: 忽然之间，某道灵光予以你启迪，你看见那些纹路竟然组合出——{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Suddenly, a spark of revelation strikes — you see the patterns rearrange into the name {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你甚至能从即将消逝的光芒中感受到那股问好的意思。
t_en: You can even feel a sense of salutation from the fading light.

t_ch: 身着绣金长袍的史官无动于衷，无人可知此刻你眼中的奇迹。
t_en: The scribe in gilded robe appears indifferent. No one else knows the miracle before your eyes at this moment.

t_ch: 你凝视着圣烛中升降的金色流体，壁画上的图案在光影交错中若隐若现，却再也没有出现过你的名字。
t_en: You gaze at the golden liquid rising and falling within the holy candle. The images on the mural flicker in the shifting light, but your name does not appear again.

t_ch: 这座圣烛坐落在左偏殿的门廊旁。在圣烛流体的光晕下，那些形似秤盘与沙漏的花纹若隐若现，呈现出一种奇特的韵律。
t_en: This holy candle is placed next to the entrance of the left side hall. Under the halo of the holy candle liquid, patterns resembling scalepans and hourglasses come into view intermittently to a peculiar rhythm.

t_ch: 不出意料的，你注意到底座上有一个精巧的开关装置。
t_en: As expected, you notice a finely crafted switch at the base.

t_ch: 熄灭圣烛。
t_en: Extinguish the holy candle.

t_ch: 在你触碰开关的瞬间，流淌的金色液体渐渐沉寂。你的意识也随着金色一同逝去……
t_en: The moment you touch the switch, the flowing golden liquid gradually stills. Your consciousness fades away along with the gold...

t_ch: …刚刚那是…被人袭击了？当你回过神来，你已经被好心人安置于此处了。
t_en: ...Were you just... attacked by someone? As you come to, you find yourself placed here by a kind soul.

t_ch: …还是说你一直坐在这里，未曾离开？刚才你凑近圣烛的经历不过是一时的想象。
t_en: ...Or have you been sitting here the entire time, never having left? Was your encounter with the holy candle merely a fleeting illusion?

t_ch: 你不得而知，毕竟在翁法罗斯记忆总是包含着奇迹的成分。
t_en: You cannot say for sure. After all, in Amphoreus, memory always holds a touch of the miraculous.

t_ch: 你再次操控圣烛，金色流体在装置中起伏。你的意识不再悄然离开，它清晰地留在此刻。
t_en: You manipulate the holy candle once more and the golden liquid rises and falls inside the device. This time, your consciousness holds its ground firmly in the present.

t_ch: 毫无疑问，你曾在故事里见过这个庞大的木制道具，瞧它那高高昂起的头颅，以及宽广到足以装进好几个人的空洞腹部——所有特征都与你的认知相符。
t_en: No doubt you recognize this massive wooden prop from tales of old — its proud head held high, its cavernous belly large enough to hold several people — every detail matches the stories you know.

t_ch: 你不明白它为何会出现在这里。能勉强与这匹木马扯上关系的唯有{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}，可命运三相殿早已荒废，那位半神同样不在此处。
t_en: You cannot understand why this would appear here. This wooden horse could only feasibly be connected to {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}, but the Temple of the Three Fates has long since fallen into ruin, and that demigod is nowhere to be seen.

t_ch: 你左右张望，都没能见到任何人影。既然如此，恐怕只能靠你来一探究竟了——
t_en: You look around you, but there is no one in sight. It seems that it is up to you to investigate...

t_ch: （钻进木马的肚子里）
t_en: (Enter the horse's stomach)

t_ch: 这不是无名客该做的事！
t_en: This is not something a Nameless should be doing!

t_ch: 是吗？可想想那些被你翻过的垃圾桶、反复研究的路灯，还有不知道砸了多少个的盾牌…真的不打算钻进去看看吗？
t_en: Really? Just think about all of those trash cans you've flipped over, all of those street lamps you've investigated over and over, the countless shields you've smashed... Are you really not going to go inside and take a look?

t_ch: （钻进木马的肚子里）
t_en: (Enter the horse's stomach)

t_ch: 还是算了吧。
t_en: Never mind.

t_ch: 要是{RUBY_B#大地之泰坦}吉奥里亚{RUBY_E#}在场，你的勤勉与节制绝对能获得它的嘉奖。可惜它并未回应…真令人遗憾。
t_en: If {RUBY_B#Earth Titan}Georios{RUBY_E#} were here, your diligence and self-control would have no doubt been commended. Unfortunately, there was no response... That's truly unfortunate.

t_ch: 木马里宽敞而幽暗，让你睡意茫茫…但眼皮打架时，眼球却被内壁的一行文字夺去了注意力——
t_en: The interior of the wooden horse is dark and spacious, lulling you into drowsiness... but as you begin to nod off, your eyes catch a line of text etched on the inner wall —

t_ch: 「神驹卡咏，带你渡过冥河，去往彼岸」
t_en: "Divine Steed Kaevo will guide you across the River of Souls to the other shore."

t_ch: 立刻起身离开。
t_en: Leave immediately.

t_ch: 做出决定后，你马上爬起，但木马却因这些微的动作而重心不稳。只听「咕咚」一声——顷刻，你已身处冥河之上。
t_en: As you make your decision, you swiftly stand, but the horse shifts beneath you, unsteady from your movement. With a loud "thud," you find yourself floating above the River of Souls.

t_ch: 什么，还有这种好事！
t_en: What? A stroke of luck!

t_ch: 或许是为了见一位朋友，又或许是单纯好奇冥河的尽头是什么，总之，你对它所阐述的内容十分向往。
t_en: Maybe you're just eager to see a friend, or maybe you're simply curious where the River of Souls leads, but either way, you are filled with anticipation.

t_ch: 伴随着雀跃的心情，你在木马中咕噜噜打滚，而它也不负众望，随着你的手舞足蹈一点点挪动着位置——最后，「咕咚」一声滑进了冥河。
t_en: With excitement bubbling inside, you roll around inside the horse, and true to form, it shifts gently with your every movement — until, with another "thud," it slides into the River of Souls.

t_ch: 管他呢，先睡一觉吧。
t_en: Who cares? Nap time.

t_ch: 你沉沉睡去，你醒了过来。四周静悄悄的，只有水声荡漾——猜猜看，发生了什么？
t_en: You drift into a deep sleep, only to wake to an eerie silence, save for the gentle lapping of water. Guess what happened?

t_ch: 希望你没有晕船的问题。
t_en: Let's hope you're not seasick.

t_ch: 木马外表看上去毫无机关，但你却可以明显感受到，有股神秘的推力在让你们缓缓前进。看来「渡河」云云并非虚言——雅努斯的神力，翁法罗斯人的第一款水上发动机。
t_en: The wooden horse appears to have no mechanism, but you can feel an unseen force gently propelling you forward. It seems the "crossing" is no mere metaphor — Janus' divine power, the first water engine of Amphoreus.

t_ch: 事已至此，你只好抛开无谓思绪，享受起了这「有如转生般迷人」的旅途。
t_en: At this point, there's nothing to do but let go of any needless thoughts and enjoy this "mesmerizing journey, like a rebirth."

t_ch: 与列车的跃迁不同，这古旧的驳船并未通电，甚至连光照都不足，实属无聊，你决定——
t_en: Unlike the Express' warp jump, this old barge isn't powered by electricity, and the lighting is scarce — boredom sets in, so you decide —

t_ch: 拿冥河水洗脚。
t_en: To wash your feet in the River of Souls.

t_ch: 友人的话语闪过脑海——
t_en: A friend's warning flashes through your mind —

t_ch: 「…会死的」
t_en: "...You'll die."

t_ch: ——你重新穿上了鞋。
t_en: ...You slip your shoes back on.

t_ch: 给朋友们发短信。
t_en: Send a message to friends.

t_ch: 「猜猜我现在在哪？」——发送。
t_en: "Guess where I am right now?" — Send.

t_ch: 嘟嘟。消息发送失败的提示音让你有些沮丧。很遗憾，万帷网是奥赫玛区域限定。
t_en: Beep beep. The message fails to send, and you feel a pang of frustration. Unfortunately, the World Wound Web is region-locked to Okhema.

t_ch: 不知过了多久——
t_en: How much time has passed, you wonder —

t_ch: 当你开始为旅途过久该如何筹备饮食而忧心忡忡的时候，伴随着「喀啷」一声，马身剧烈地摇晃了一下，随后便如大地般沉静了下来——你意识到，「彼岸」就在眼前。
t_en: Just as you begin to worry about what provisions to prepare for the journey, there's a loud "clank," and the wooden horse jerks violently for a moment before settling back down as quietly as the earth beneath you. That's when it hits you — the "other shore" is right in front of you.

t_ch: 将门板降下，你向未知的未来迈出了第一步。
t_en: With a click, the door falls open, and you step forward, ready to face the unknown future.

t_ch: 「开拓」的时间到了！
t_en: It's time for "trailblazing"!

t_ch: 能见到那个人了吗！
t_en: Can I finally meet that person!?

t_ch: 眼前是你钻进木马的地方——准确地说，不完全是，你身处码头的另一侧。
t_en: Before you stands the spot you entered the wooden horse — well, not exactly, you've landed on the opposite side of the dock.

t_ch: 神性的声音在风中响起，随后缓缓消散——只余一枚硬币在你手中。
t_en: A divine voice rises with the wind and gradually fades — leaving only a single coin in your hand.

t_ch: 「洋流剧变，航线暂停——」
t_en: "Ocean currents shifting drastically, route suspended —"

t_ch: 负责摆渡的木马，在冥河河畔的微风中轻轻摇曳，目前暂不营业。
t_en: The ferryman wooden horse gently sways in the breeze along the River of Souls, now out of service.

t_ch: 毫无疑问，你曾在故事里见过这个庞大的木制道具，瞧它那高高昂起的头颅，以及宽广到足以装进好几个人的空洞腹部——所有特征都与你的认知相符。
t_en: No doubt you recognize this massive wooden prop from tales of old — its proud head held high, its cavernous belly large enough to hold several people — every detail matches the stories you know.

t_ch: 你，你有什么事？这木马是我的东西，没什么事别靠过来，碰坏了怎么办？
t_en: You — what are you up to? This wooden horse is mine. Don't get too close unless you want to break it.

t_ch: 我在别的地方也见过它……
t_en: I've seen it somewhere else...

t_ch: 好好好，我这就离开。
t_en: Alright. I'll get going.

t_ch: 别，别的我不管。这个确实属于我，我花大价钱从收藏家那买的所有权，有权契的。
t_en: No, no, that's not the issue. This really is mine — I paid a lot to buy it from a collector, and I have the deed to prove it.

t_ch: 为什么要买这个？
t_en: Why did you buy it?

t_ch: 你对废弃车厢有没有兴趣啊？
t_en: Are you interested in abandoned wagons or something?

t_ch: 别，别拿我跟那些搞古玩的怪人混为一谈。
t_en: No, no, don't lump me in with those antique weirdos.

t_ch: 这是我存…不是，这是刻法勒的祭器，很尊贵的。没事的话，我先走了…别，别碰。
t_en: This is my... No, this is a ritual vessel of Kephale, very valuable. Anyway, I'll be leaving now... just don't touch it.

t_ch: 私人所有的木马，这可是头一次见…等等，里面好像有动静？
t_en: A private wooden horse, never seen before... wait, did you hear something inside?

t_ch: 咦，我刚刚不是在家里的仓库吗？{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，这是哪啊？
t_en: Huh, wasn't I just in my home's warehouse, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}? Where is this?

t_ch: 黎明云崖。
t_en: Dawncloud.

t_ch: 刻法勒的旁边。
t_en: By Kephale's side.

t_ch: 呀，到上面来啦！这么说，那些东西一定是{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}你送来的吧！
t_en: Oh, I came all the way up here! So, those things must have been delivered by you, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}, right?

t_ch: 每隔一段时间，仓库里就会莫名其妙多出来一些财物，妈妈总说是{RUBY_B#浪漫之泰坦}墨涅塔{RUBY_E#}的眷顾，但我一直都不相信。
t_en: Every so often, some mysterious objects appear in the warehouse. Mom always says it's {RUBY_B#Romance Titan}Mnestia{RUBY_E#}'s blessings, but I've never really believed it.

t_ch: 不过，自从我爸爸去世后，家里一直有些拮据，这些东西真的帮大忙了！
t_en: But since Dad passed, things have been tight at home, and these items have really helped!

t_ch: 其实不是我送的。
t_en: Actually, it wasn't me who sent them.

t_ch: 是吗？好吧。那会是谁呢？
t_en: Really? Then who could it have been?

t_ch: 刚刚有个叔叔你认识吗？
t_en: Did you know that man here just now?

t_ch: 叔叔？谁啊？不清楚。
t_en: A man here? Who? I don't know anyone like that.

t_ch: 咦？木马里有声音！难道是我妈妈也来了？
t_en: Huh? It's coming from inside the wooden horse! Has my mom also arrived?

t_ch: 这是…云崖外侧吗？果然如此，本次行动成功，我这就回去申请逮捕令……
t_en: Is this... outside Dawncloud? Just as expected, the mission was a success. I'll head back to apply for an arrest warrant...

t_ch: 咦？你是「金织」女士的客人，还有这孩子是……
t_en: Huh? You're Lady Goldweaver's guest, and this child is...

t_ch: ……你来一下！
t_en: ...Over this way, please!

t_ch: …实不相瞒，我是元老院的一名督查。我们怀疑一位议员长期收受贿赂，却一直找不到证据。
t_en: ...To be honest, I'm an inspector from the Council of Elders. We've been suspecting a legislator of accepting bribes for years, but we could never find solid evidence.

t_ch: 这木马曾是由雅努萨波利斯派遣至此的灵器，于雅努斯所掌管，如今虽然破败，却还留有远距离输送的权能……
t_en: This wooden horse was once a divine instrument sent by Janusopolis under Janus' rule. Though it's now in ruins, it still has the power to send things across long distances...

t_ch: 我们发现，数月前，嫌疑人买下了这物件，堂而皇之摆在城中，实在有些可疑，便组织了调查…结果不出所料。
t_en: We discovered that the suspect bought it months ago and displayed it openly in the city. Naturally, this raised our suspicions, and we began an investigation... And the result is as we expected.

t_ch: 他靠这木马将多数来历不明的财产移动到了远方…虽说「最危险的地方就是最安全的地方」，但把贼窝设在卫兵哨站，这就有些过分了……
t_en: He used this wooden horse to move most of his ill-gotten wealth far away... While "the most dangerous place is often the safest," setting up his den at a guard post seems a bit too obvious...

t_ch: …叔叔，你也是从我家的仓库过来的吗？
t_en: ...Did you come from my house's warehouse too?

t_ch: 咦？啊，这个，这个啊……
t_en: Huh? About that... Uhh...

t_ch: *使了个眼色*
t_en: *gives you a look*

t_ch: 是这位叔叔给你送的东西。
t_en: It was him who delivered the items to you.

t_ch: 真的吗！谢谢叔叔！咦，我好像在我家附近看见过你，你在追求我妈妈吗？
t_en: Really!? Thank you, mister! Hey, I think I've seen you near my house — do you like my mom?

t_ch: 不对，那个，呃，你就当是这样吧，哈哈…来，我送你回家，路上我给你讲讲……
t_en: No, wait, uh, let's just leave it at that, haha... Come, let me walk you home, and I'll explain on the way...

t_ch: 其实这个木马通向好多地方。
t_en: Actually, this wooden horse leads to many places.

t_ch: 这样啊……这么说，是有位大善人一直在给大家送东西吗？
t_en: I see... So, is there a kind person who's been sending things to us?

t_ch: 嗯，没错，而且不止一个人呢。那位{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}也送了很多东西给需要的人，不过他们之后可能会有些忙…我先送你回家，路上我们可以聊聊……
t_en: Yes, that's right, and it's not just one person. That {F#big sister}{M#big brother} has sent a lot of things to people in need too, though they might be a bit busy afterward... I'll take you home, and we can chat on the way...

t_ch: 帮大忙了，感谢。愿刻法勒庇佑你，朋友。
t_en: Thanks again for your help. May Kephale watch over your back, my friend.

t_ch: 安静的木马，曾发生在它身上的小插曲，除你之外无人知晓。
t_en: The quiet wooden horse. The small incident that took place upon it is known only to you.

t_ch: 天空堡垒已经倾颓，滚烫的熔金池水如蛛网般在废墟中溢流。风堇在行将崩塌的晨昏之眼中艰难穿行，来到破碎的天象画壁前——
t_en: The Sky Fortress has crumbled, and molten gold spills across the ruins, spreading like webs. Hyacine struggles through the collapsing Eye of Twilight, finally arriving at the broken celestial mural.

t_ch: 塞涅俄丝大人最后告诉我，天空的权柄已经与艾格勒的神躯融合，一同被熔铸进了天象画壁之中。
t_en: Lady Seliose told me at the end that the authority of Sky had fused with Aquila's divine body, and both were molten into the celestial mural.

t_ch: 现在情况紧急，没时间慢悠悠地剥离天空的神权了。
t_en: Time is of the essence — there's no time to slowly extract Sky's authority.

t_ch: 最快的办法…就是把自己也镌刻进天象画壁，与艾格勒融为一体，支撑起天空……
t_en: The quickest way... is to insert myself into the mural, merge with Aquila, and support the Sky...

t_ch: 哈哈，简直就像昏光庭院代代相承的天空英雄传说，在我身上成真了一样呢。
t_en: Haha... It's almost like the legendary tales of the Sky heroes, passed down through generations in the Twilight Courtyard, are coming true through me.

t_ch: 虽然那听上去很痛…但是，最后印在画壁中的我，一定展露着笑容。
t_en: It might sound painful... But in the end, I will surely be smiling even as a carving in the mural.

t_ch: 因为…我能治愈天空。
t_en: Because... I can heal the Sky.

t_ch: 这边马上就不安全了，大家收拾收拾东西，赶紧和圣城卫士一起去避难处吧！
t_en: This place is no longer safe. Everyone, pack up and go to the shelter with the Holy City Guards!

t_ch: 但是，克莱门汀小姐，这是你和风堇小姐好不容易才选定的昏光庭院新址。
t_en: But Miss Clementine... this is the new location for the Twilight Courtyard that you and Miss Hyacine spent so much time selecting.

t_ch: 圣城卫士中有不少人都接受过风堇小姐的救治，我们愿意分出一些兵力来保护她最后留下的庭院……
t_en: Miss Hyacine has treated many of us Holy City Guards. We are willing to send some people to guard this courtyard she left behind...

t_ch: 谢谢你们的好意。只是，新昏光庭院地处宅巷，一旦被黑潮怪物包围，你们的处境会非常危险。
t_en: Thank you for your kindness. However, the new Twilight Courtyard is in a residential alley. If it gets surrounded by the black tide monsters, you will be all in grave danger.

t_ch: 哪怕风堇小姐在场，她也一定不会愿意让你们身处险境。
t_en: If Miss Hyacine is here, she would never want you to be in harm's way.

t_ch: 可是，这些漂亮的装饰…一定也会被黑潮怪物们毁掉的。
t_en: But... these beautiful decorations... they'll be destroyed by the monsters.

t_ch: 没关系的。昏光庭院…并不是个稳固的家园。光是风堇小姐本人，就亲眼目睹了庭院的两次解离。
t_en: It’s alright. The Twilight Courtyard was never a stable home. Miss Hyacine herself has witnessed its dissolution twice.

t_ch: 为了重建庭院，风堇小姐确实花了很多心思。无论在树庭还是奥赫玛，当她为昏光庭院选址的时候，总是会跑遍每一处角落，只为找到环境最优美的一隅。
t_en: She truly put in a lot of effort to rebuild it. Whenever she was choosing a location for the Twilight Courtyard, whether in the Grove or Okhema, she'd search every corner, just to find the most beautiful spot.

t_ch: 但是她或许并不知道，我们大家都觉得，只要风堇小姐在的地方，就是洒满暖光的圣庭呢！
t_en: But perhaps she isn't aware that — We all believe that wherever Miss Hyacine is, the warm sanctuary will be!

t_ch: …是啊。说的夸张些，哪怕昏光庭院建在重渊，风堇小姐也会把那片黑漆漆的地方照亮吧。
t_en: ...That's true. To put it bluntly, even if the Twilight Courtyard were built in the Abyss, Miss Hyacine would still light up that dark place.

t_ch: 克莱门汀小姐，也请你先行撤离吧，我来帮你搬走这些医疗用具。
t_en: Miss Clementine, please evacuate this place. I'll help you move these medical supplies.

t_ch: …顺带，我还是想捎带一些亮晶晶的装饰品，哈哈……
t_en: ...By the way, I'd like to take some shiny decorations with me, haha...

t_ch: 巴特鲁斯，你好端端藏墙里干嘛？
t_en: Bartholos, why are you hiding in the wall?

t_ch: 嘿，这不是怕大姐头又把我拽去啥危险的地方嘛。现在这奥赫玛可不太平，咱一个小贼灵可不想掺和进啥大风大浪。
t_en: Hey, I'm just trying to avoid you dragging me to dangerous places. Okhema's not safe right now, and as a little Spirithief, I want no part in big troubles.

t_ch: 你和我打了这么久交道，就是天塌了我都不能诓你呀。今天，我是来给你送礼的。
t_en: You and I have worked together for so long, you know I'd never fool you. I've come to bring you a gift today.

t_ch: 喏，这可是我从黎明云崖顺来的天下第一宝物，接好了。
t_en: Here, I took the world's best treasure from Dawncloud. Catch.

t_ch: 哇，赛飞儿大姐头，你来真的呀！嘿嘿，让我看看里头——
t_en: Wow, boss Cipher, you're serious! Hehe, let me see what's inside...

t_ch: 哟，胆大包天的贼灵巴特鲁斯，你怎么发抖了？
t_en: Well, well, daring Spirithief Bartholos, why are you shaking?

t_ch: 天哪，赛飞儿大姐头，这这这，这我可不敢收啊……
t_en: Oh my gosh, boss Cipher. I... I can't possibly accept this...

t_ch: 据我所知，很久以前你也试着偷过黎明机器吧？还有艾格勒的眼睛，法吉娜的酒盏……
t_en: I know you've tried to steal the Dawn Device before, as well as Aquila's eye, Phagousa's wine cup...

t_ch: 现在吃颗<color=#dbc291ff>刻法勒的火种</color>算什么呀。
t_en: What's wrong with eating <color=#dbc291ff>Kephale's Coreflame</color> on top of all those?

t_ch: 赛飞儿大姐头，这可不是假货…您就别消遣我了，从哪儿来还哪儿去吧，我先溜为敬……
t_en: Boss Cipher, this is the real deal... Please, leave me alone with this. Just return it where it came from... I'll take my leave...

t_ch: 哎哎，别想逃。不跟你开玩笑了——我要出趟远门，危险得很，弄不好就回不来了。
t_en: Don't even think about running. Lemme be straight with you — I'm going on a super dangerous journey far away. I might never make it back.

t_ch: 怎么可能呢，这世上没人跑得过翻飞之币。
t_en: No way. No one in this world can outrun the Coin of Whimsy.

t_ch: 但愿如此吧。可万一真出了意外……
t_en: I sure hope so... But if something does happen...

t_ch: 我需要你保护好这颗火种。如果你看到天空出现异样，不要犹豫，用你最快的速度找到救世小子，把火种交给他。
t_en: Then I need you to protect this Coreflame. If you notice anything unusual in the sky, then go find the Deliverer boy as fast as you can and give him this Coreflame. Don't delay for even a fraction of a second.

t_ch: …大姐头，咱们只是俩小偷，天真塌了也轮不到咱们来扛呀。不如听我的，咱们有多远跑多远，凭诡计的神力，就算世界真的毁灭了……
t_en: ... Boss, we're just two teeny thieves. Even if the sky falls for real, we aren't the ones who need to carry it. Why not run as far as we can? With the power of Trickery, even if the world gets destroyed...

t_ch: ——奥赫玛早就陷入永夜了。让黎明机器继续燃烧的，正是诡计的神力。
t_en: ...Okhema has been in Evernight since centuries ago. Trickery's divine power is what has kept the Dawn Device burning.

t_ch: 事到如今，我不得不向你透露这个欺世之谎。我们早就背负着世界的命运，没有独善其身的余地。
t_en: At this moment in time, I have no choice but to reveal this grand lie to you. We've been shouldering the fate of this world all this time. There is no way for us to come out of this in one piece.

t_ch: …你是说…我们的力量，诡计的力量…骗过了这世上所有的白痴、恶徒和灾难，拯救了翁法罗斯？
t_en: ...You mean... our divine power, the power of Trickery... has deceived all the world's fools, villains, and even the very calamities themselves? We saved Amphoreus?

t_ch: 没错。巴特鲁斯，这不正是你想要的吗？最孱弱的影子，却负担起此世的黎明……
t_en: That's right. Isn't this what you've always wanted, Bartholos? You are the weakest shadow, yet you now bear the only dawn of this world...

t_ch: 胆大包天的扎格列斯本尊，你怎么又发抖了？
t_en: Great god Zagreus, infinitely bold and reckless, why are you trembling again?

t_ch: …嘿嘿，这是兴奋的抖擞，大姐头。不出意外的话，这是我们最后的骗局了吧？
t_en: ...Heh. I'm shaking from excitement. Boss, If all goes well, this should be our final trick, right?

t_ch: 正是如此。不过，我还是要警告你，要是你敢掖着火种临阵脱逃……
t_en: Yes. But I must warn you — if you dare hide the Coreflame and run when the time comes...

t_ch: 到了下辈子，我一定把你丢冥河里喂鱼！
t_en: I'll feed you to the fish in the River of Souls in our next life!

t_ch: 大工匠，你怎么来了？黎明云崖现在很危险，黑潮怪物纷纷从低处向云崖汇集，已经有不少圣城卫士战死了……
t_en: Grand Craftsman, what brings you here? The Dawncloud is perilous right now. The black tide monsters are gathering from below, and many of the Holy City Guards have already fallen...

t_ch: 支援前线，尽一份力，我来。
t_en: To support the front lines and do my part, I'm here.

t_ch: 上好的武器，我带来了。保卫奥赫玛，我与诸位勇士一同。
t_en: Fine weapons, I've brought them here. To protect Okhema, I stand with the brave warriors.

t_ch: 是大工匠亲手打制的神兵利器！我早就想买一把了，只是预算一直不够，这下得偿所愿了，就是牺牲了也值得！
t_en: These are marvels of weaponry forged by the Grand Craftsman himself! I've always wanted one, but never had the budget. Now, I get my wish — even if I die, it will be worth it!

t_ch: 别说傻话！你还年轻，待会儿老老实实跟在我们身后，别傻乎乎一个人冲锋。
t_en: Stop talking nonsense! You're still young. Stay behind us later and don't charge ahead alone.

t_ch: 大工匠，我们感谢你雪中送炭。只是，你现在的身体状况，实在不适合以战士的身份留在这里啊。
t_en: Grand Craftsman, we're grateful for your timely help. But... your current condition isn't suited for you to stay here as a warrior.

t_ch: 并非战士，我向来。但是现在，我亦应抗争。
t_en: I am no warrior. I never was. But now, I too must resist.

t_ch: 但是……
t_en: But...

t_ch: 泽弗，执拗是大工匠的老脾气了。当初尼卡多利袭城，哪次他没有挺身而出？
t_en: Zeph, stubbornness has always been the Grand Craftsman's trademark. When Nikador attacked the city, didn't he always step forward?

t_ch: 事到如今，我们也没办法把紧握着武器的大工匠硬搬回云石市集啊。
t_en: He's clutching his weapon, you see? We can't just drag him back to the Marmoreal Market.

t_ch: 大工匠，白厄大人先前委托我们前去贵客们的私人浴宫待命，无奈我们一直抽不开身。
t_en: Grand Craftsman, Lord Phainon had tasked us with waiting for further instructions at the guests' private bath chamber, but we still haven't had the time to carry out that order.

t_ch: 将战事托付给你，我们很惭愧。但…请你替我们带领卫士与黑潮战斗吧！
t_en: I'm sorry for entrusting the battle to you, but... please lead the guards to fight against the black tide!

t_ch: 去吧，完成使命，你们的。
t_en: Go. Fulfill the duty, your duty.

t_ch: …大工匠，请务必照看好自己！
t_en: ...Grand Craftsman, please take care of yourself!

t_ch: …在靠近，敌人。举起武器，勇士们！
t_en: ...Enemies approaching. Raise your weapons, warriors!

t_ch: 诺杜斯先生，我在周围打听了一遭，即便是刻法勒的祭司，也对黎明机器的突变毫无头绪……
t_en: Mr. Noldus... I've asked around, but even Kephale's priests are baffled by the Dawn Device's sudden change...

t_ch: 怎会如此…大祭司大人呢？你可知她现在身在何处？
t_en: How could this be... Where is the High Priest now?

t_ch: 缇里西庇俄丝大人…正在引导奥赫玛的民众疏散，前往广场暂避。
t_en: Lady Tribios is leading the citizens to evacuate to the square, seeking temporary shelter.

t_ch: …维尔图斯啊。看来，这就是我们的最后一个晨昏时了。
t_en: ...Virtus. Looks like this is our final day in this world.

t_ch: 您这是什么话！不，当务之急，先和我一起去安全的地方躲一躲吧。
t_en: What? No! The priority is for us to find shelter in a safe place.

t_ch: 永夜之下，还有哪里是安全的？新世界吗？
t_en: Where can we find safety in the Evernight? The "New World"?

t_ch: 您…还是对「再创世」的愿景有所不安。
t_en: You... You are still uneasy about the vision of the Era Nova.

t_ch: 是啊。但事到如今，我不会多言。复述，教典第三十七章，第四行。
t_en: Yes, but I won't say much more at this point. Now, recite the <unbreak>37</unbreak>th chapter of the scriptures. Fourth line.

t_ch: 「若门后的景色美好，却无人能够跨越，也不妨认定那就是美好之处。」
t_en: "If the scenery behind the door is charming yet none can step into it, then it may still be a place of beauty."

t_ch: 诚然，身为命运泰坦的祭司，至少我们自身，不该阻碍向往命运之人的热忱。
t_en: Indeed, as the priest of the Fate Titans, we must not stand in the way of those who long for their destiny.

t_ch: 自从迁来奥赫玛，您的想法确实改变了不少。我想其他祭司们应该会笑着议论纷纷吧。
t_en: Your views have changed quite a bit since you arrived in Okhema. I imagine the other priests are probably laughing and discussing this behind your back.

t_ch: 哼，罢了。我们去广场吧。事情发生得太过突然，民众恐怕仍处于慌乱中。祭司的任务，便是在命运到来前，成为人们的依靠——
t_en: Hmph, enough. Let's head to the Plaza. The citizens are probably still panicking with how fast everything happened. As priests, we must offer them support before the destiny arrives —

t_ch: 二位，快离开那！！
t_en: You two, get away from there!!

t_ch: …黑潮造物？！维尔图斯，别管我！快走！！
t_en: ...Black tide creatures!? Virtus, don't worry about me! Run!!

t_ch: 当心！
t_en: Watch out!

t_ch: 记忆戛然而止，伴随着一丝凉意。
t_en: The memory ends abruptly, leaving only a cold chill.

t_ch: 呼，呼…这就是传说中的黑潮怪物？没想到我居然能干掉一个…莫非我其实有成为黄金裔英雄的潜力？
t_en: Phew... So this is the legendary black tide monster? I can't believe I actually managed to take one down... Could I have the potential to become a hero among the Chrysos Heirs?

t_ch: 嘶，好疼…果然还是不能得意忘形啊…我的血也是红色的，看来英雄的结局并不适合我。
t_en: Hiss... That hurts... I really let my guard down, didn't I? And my blood is red... so I suppose a hero's end isn't meant to be mine after all.

t_ch: 好可怕的伤口，我恐怕命不久矣了吧……
t_en: This wound is too deep... I don't have a lot of time left...

t_ch: 明明说好去天外之界看看的，没想到最后要死在地上，真是辱没了探险家的名号……
t_en: I was going to see the worlds beyond the sky... but now I'm going to die on the ground. What a disgrace to the title of adventurer...

t_ch: 唉，以前的生命花园是多么热闹，偏偏这会儿冷清得要死…我这满腹遗言都没人可以倾诉。
t_en: Ahhh, the Garden of Life used to be so lively. Why does it have to be so quiet right now... I have so much to say before I go, yet no one to share them with.

t_ch: 又或者…这是我此生仅有的机会？
t_en: Or maybe... this is my only chance?

t_ch: 既然黎明机器已经熄灭，就说明异乡人阁下他们成功了，艾格勒的「天空诅咒」此刻肯定也不存在了……
t_en: If the Dawn Device has been extinguished, then it means the outlander succeeded. Aquila's curse is surely gone now...

t_ch: 如果是现在的话，或许真能触及天外之界也说不定！
t_en: Maybe I can actually reach the world beyond the sky now!

t_ch: 我的「羽翼」…被他们放在哪里了？
t_en: My "wings"... Where did they put them?

t_ch: …啊，在那儿！圣城守卫还挺能藏。希望我有足够的力气爬过去……
t_en: ...Ah, over there! The Holy City Guards sure know how to hide things well. I hope I have enough strength to crawl over...

t_ch: 呼…你可以的，达米亚诺斯，你可是个探险家。
t_en: Huff... Huff... You can do this, Damionis. You're an adventurer.

t_ch: 你看着达米亚诺斯骑上飞天坛递，向着断崖发起了冲锋，仿佛挑战巨人的骑士。
t_en: You see Damionis mount the Flying Amphora and charge toward the cliff, like a knight challenging giants.

t_ch: 他的身影一转眼便消失在了视野尽头，你不确定那坛递最后有没有升上天空……
t_en: In the blink of an eye, his figure disappears beyond the horizon, and you're unsure if the amphora ever ascended into the sky...

t_ch: 但无论这次尝试成功与否，他大概都不会再回到奥赫玛了。
t_en: Regardless of whether this attempt succeeds, he probably won't be coming back to Okhema.

t_ch: 九…十…十一……
t_en: Nine... Ten... Eleven...

t_ch: 嗯…神速硬币还剩十二枚。只是帮救世小子他们拖延时间的话，肯定够用了吧？
t_en: There are <unbreak>12</unbreak> Godspeed Coins left. It should be enough if I'm only buying time for the little Deliverer, right?

t_ch: 大姐头，你…你真的要这么做吗？下定决心，不会反悔了？
t_en: Boss, are... are you really going to do this? You're absolutely sure? No takebacks?

t_ch: 哈，怎么，你害怕了？
t_en: Hah, what's wrong? You scared?

t_ch: 没有，我只是担心…你也知道的，那个黑衣服的家伙强得可怕——
t_en: No, I'm just worried... You know, that fellow in black is ridiculously strong—

t_ch: 你怕死吗，巴特鲁斯？
t_en: Are you scared of death, Bartholos?

t_ch: …欸？怎么突然问这个？
t_en: ...Hm? Why are you asking this all of a sudden?

t_ch: 嘿…没什么，就是想告诉你——我可不怕。不过就是去和那位蜗居公主团聚罢了，能有多吓人？
t_en: Heh, it's nothing, I just want to tell you that I'm not. I'll simply reunite with Princess Homebody, that's all. What's there to be scared of?

t_ch: 我怕的是…自己最后的结局对这个世界而言毫无价值，就和我的那些族人一样。
t_en: What I'm afraid of is... that my final moments won't be of any value to this world, just like my people.

t_ch: 我…我也一样啊，大姐头。如果不能和你一起施展一次最伟大的骗术…「诡计」一定也死不瞑目呀！
t_en: I... I feel the same way, Boss. If I don't get to perform the greatest deception... "Trickery" with you once, I won't be able to rest in peace!

t_ch: 嘿，就知道你会这么说。来吧，按照我们的计划——你可要好好留在缇宝和缇宁阿姐的身边哪。
t_en: Heh, I knew you'd say that. Come on, just follow our plan — you have to stay with big sis Tribbie and Trinnon.

t_ch: 记住：你现在只是一枚信封，可不许出声吓唬她们哪。
t_en: Remember: you're merely a letter now, don't make any noise to scare them.

t_ch: 我…一定不会辜负你的信任，大姐头！
t_en: I... I won't disappoint you, Boss!

t_ch: 呵…真没想到，我们最后的诡计，竟然建立在对彼此毫无保留的信任之上……
t_en: Ha... Who would have thought that our final trick would be built on unconditional mutual trust...

t_ch: 真讽刺呀，扎格列斯。
t_en: How exhilarating, Zagreus.

t_ch: 昏光庭院的风堇，你心中最温柔的黄金裔。她已将天空缝补，向大地展露着治愈的笑容。现在你之所见，只是记忆复现出的残像。
t_en: Hyacine of the Twilight Courtyard, the gentlest Chrysos Heir in your heart. She has mended the sky, and offers a healing smile to the earth. What you now behold is merely an afterimage reproduced from memory.

t_ch: 呀，灰宝！今天阳光特别不错，谢谢刻法勒，你也是来晒太阳的吧。
t_en: Ah, Grayie! What a gorgeous sunny day, thank Kephale. You're here to soak up some sun too, aren't you?

t_ch: 还是说…你是有什么烦心事，想要找人倾诉一下？
t_en: Or perhaps... Is there something on your mind that you want to talk about?

t_ch: 你是最温柔最伟大的医师！
t_en: You are the most gentle and magnificent healer ever!

t_ch: 记忆的残像似乎晃了晃，过往对话的回忆因其承载的情感之重杂糅在一起……
t_en: The fragment of memory seems to waver — memories of words once spoken blend together due to the emotion they contain...

t_ch: 啊…谢谢你的夸奖！
t_en: Ah... thank you for your compliment!

t_ch: 我只是一名医师，即便拥有了力量，也无法变得和其他半神一样强大。
t_en: I am merely a healer. Even with this power, I cannot match the strength of other demigods.

t_ch: 但若能降下温柔的虹光，守护尽可能多的生灵……
t_en: But if I can bring down gentle rainbow light and protect as many lives as possible...

t_ch: 我也算实现了自己的理想呀。
t_en: Then I will have truly realized my dream.

t_ch: 这下你也成为英雄了。
t_en: Now you've become a hero too.

t_ch: 哈哈…几乎每段史诗都以英雄的功业作结。
t_en: Haha... almost every epic tale concludes with a hero's great deeds.

t_ch: 但我想做的，只是站在英雄们身后，在金色的浪潮将世界推向新生时，尽己所能疗愈每一个可能会溺亡的普通人。
t_en: But what I truly wish is to stand behind the heroes, healing every ordinary person who might drown when the golden tide pushes the world toward its rebirth.

t_ch: 给我留个彩虹桥吧，还想见你。
t_en: Save me a rainbow bridge, I still want to see you again.

t_ch: 记忆的残像沉默不语，因为这是不曾发生过的对话。
t_en: The shadow of the memory remains silent, for this conversation never truly occurred.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅，浪漫之半神、黄金裔的领袖，已为逐火之旅燃尽了最后一缕金辉。现在你之所见，只是记忆复现出的残像。
t_en: Aglaea, the demigod of Romance, leader of the Chrysos Heirs, has burned her last golden radiance for the Flame-Chase Journey. What you now behold is merely an afterimage reproduced from memory.

t_ch: 隔着百步之距时，金线就已预知贵客的到来。{NICKNAME}阁下，想在此休息会儿吗？
t_en: The golden thread had already foreseen your arrival when you were a hundred steps away, dear guest. Esteemed {NICKNAME}, have you come to rest?

t_ch: 请你评价一下自己吧。
t_en: Please evaluate yourself.

t_ch: 记忆的残像似乎晃了晃，过往对话的回忆因其承载的情感之重杂糅在一起……
t_en: The fragment of memory seems to waver — memories of words once spoken blend together due to the emotion they contain...

t_ch: 一千年的时光…我见证了十位泰坦的殒落。从最初的娇蛮无知，再到摸索着为众人引路……
t_en: In these one thousand years... I've witnessed the fall of ten Titans. From my naive arrogance at the beginning to slowly learning how to guide others...

t_ch: 希望我的离去…能筑成众人的长阶。
t_en: I hope my departure... will form steps that others may ascend.

t_ch: 白厄不会辜负你的期待。
t_en: Phainon will not disappoint you.

t_ch: 他是翁法罗斯注定的负世者，通向翁法罗斯未来的门扉。
t_en: He is the destined Worldbearer of Amphoreus, the gate to Amphoreus's future.

t_ch: 我会在西风的尽头，盼待由他开辟出的奇迹。
t_en: I will be waiting at the end of the west wind, looking forward to the miracle he will create.

t_ch: 你已经做得够多了。
t_en: You've already done enough.

t_ch: …我做得…够吗？
t_en: Have I?

t_ch: 也许我该庆幸…庆幸自己还是等来了这一刻啊。
t_en: Maybe I should be grateful... grateful that I've reached this moment.

t_ch: 我们会替你目睹再创世。
t_en: We will witness the Era Nova for you.

t_ch: 记忆的残像沉默不语，因为这是不曾发生过的对话。
t_en: The shadow of the memory remains silent, for this conversation never truly occurred.

t_ch: 止步吧卑劣残缺的胎生种，回去大地那溺爱你们的摇床。
t_en: Cease your advance, vile and flawed mammals, back to the land's pampering cradle.

t_ch: 去嘶声用你们肮脏的肺腑詈骂，去撕咬为每一粒石砾争夺。
t_en: Go screech with your dirty lungs, go fight over every pebble.

t_ch: 别再上前一步，因为此处的静寂不容亵渎。
t_en: Step no closer, for this silence tolerates no sacrilege.

t_ch: 侵占每一束阳光，汲取每一滴雨露，直到万物干枯。永不停歇，永无休止。
t_en: Claim each ray of sun, take every raindrop, until everything dries up. Never stopping, never ending.

t_ch: 沉溺在骄纵、仇恨和他人的苦痛中自得其乐。永不满足，永无尽头。
t_en: Wallow in pride, hatred, and others' pain. Never satisfied, never complete.

t_ch: 吞食大地的蛆虫，别用你们的触足玷污天空！
t_en: Earth-eating worms, don't soil the sky with your limbs!

t_ch: 用洗刷天空阴翳的沸血，去交换贯穿百目的长矛和一句箴言——
t_en: Trade boiling blood that cleanses sky's shadows for a spear through hundred eyes and this truth—

t_ch: 救赎不过是愚不可及的空想。
t_en: Redemption is but a dream foolish beyond reach.

t_ch: 可鄙的土地应当降下硫磺和盐卤。我誓要如此。
t_en: The wretched land deserves sulfur and saltwater. This I swear.

t_ch: 背叛的骗徒应当吞下焦炭和烈火。我本当如此。
t_en: The lying traitor should eat coal and fire. This I vow.

t_ch: 云层游移，雨水落下，溪涧流淌，河流逝去。
t_en: The clouds drift, the rain falls, the streams flow, the rivers pass...

t_ch: 又重新在海面升起。
t_en: ...and rise again from the sea.

t_ch: 往复的云层之下，我们忘记一切，重新开始往复的航行。
t_en: Under the cyclical clouds, we forget everything and start our endless journey anew.

t_ch: 你们为何渴求，为何呵护，为何毁坏，为何生活。
t_en: Why you yearn, why you protect, why you wreck, why you live...

t_ch: 生生世世，世世代代，为何仍在岁月的溪流里踽踽独行。
t_en: ...life after life, age after age, why still wander alone in time's river?

t_ch: 哪怕出发的缘由早已在口口相传中褪色？
t_en: Even when your journey's purpose has faded through countless retellings?

t_ch: <color=#eb4d3dff>止步吧</color>卑劣残缺的胎生种卑劣残缺██止步胎生种止步卑劣残缺█胎生种止步卑劣残缺的胎生种止步████止止卑劣残缺卑劣残缺卑劣残缺<color=#eb4d3dff>卑劣残缺胎生种</color>！

t_ch: 永无休止█永无休止——<color=#eb4d3dff>骄纵仇恨苦痛</color>永无休止，█无休止。永█████永无休止█无休止骄纵仇恨苦痛。██让我再看一眼再看███永无休止永无<color=#eb4d3dff>永不停歇永无休止永不满足永无尽头！</color>

End████ ████ █ndless pride hatred pain never cease. ██████ Let me see once more, let me see ███ ████ Endless, never-ending <color=#eb4d3dff>never stopping, never ceasing, never satisfied, never-ending!</color>

t_ch: 艾格勒示现在启目集上的话语已经彻底扭曲了……
t_en: What Aquila said in the enlightenment has been completely distorted...

t_ch: 启目集里还残存着艾格勒和塞涅俄丝的意志，但愿风堇能抵抗住这股力量的扰乱……
t_en: There are remnants of Aquila and Seliose's will left in the enlightenment. I hope Hyacine can resist this power's disruption...

t_ch: 闪烁着星光的纹路带，仿佛夜空中的绸缎。它的源头似乎是个谜。
t_en: A band of shimmering starlit patterns, like silk draped across the night sky. Its origin remains a mystery.

t_ch: 你想起星际网络上很火的各种「解压视频」，不知道拿剪刀把它剪断会不会同样解压。
t_en: You recall the popular "stress-relief videos" on the Interastral Network. You wonder if cutting it with scissors would be just as satisfying.

t_ch: 智库上记载，数十个琥珀纪前，「柔性线路板」在旧世科技产品的部件选择中占据主要地位——而这条光带看上去很是相似。
t_en: According to records in the data bank, tens of Amber Eras ago, "flexible circuit boards" were a dominant choice in the components of the Old World technology products. This glowing ribbon looks remarkably similar.

t_ch: 叶片间扑面而来的热风让人联想到列车上的控温系统，亦或是狂转的电脑风扇——或许，它们的作用一致。
t_en: The rush of hot air through the leaves is reminiscent of the temperature control systems on a train or the whirring fans of an overworked computer... perhaps they serve the same function.

t_ch: 你盯着墙上的排风口，伸手感受着气孔处传来的微风，想起了翁法罗斯的一句民谚：「艾格勒的叹息即是凡间的风暴。」里面会是哪位泰坦在呼吸吗？
t_en: You stare at the vent on the wall, reaching out to feel the faint breeze escaping from its perforations. A saying from Amphoreus comes to mind: "Aquila's sighs are the storms of the mortal realm." Could there be a Titan breathing within?

t_ch: 精美雕刻的通风口，设计与实用的结合之美证明它并非出自普通工匠之手。
t_en: The finely carved vent, a perfect blend of design and functionality, proves that it was not crafted by an ordinary artisan.

t_ch: 这路面碎得恰到好处，有种你在做梦的错觉。
t_en: The pavement is broken in just the right way, giving you the eerie sensation that you might be dreaming.

t_ch: 破碎的来路悬浮在空中，似乎并不被重力束缚。
t_en: The shattered path ahead floats in midair, seemingly unbound by gravity.

t_ch: 蓦然回首，已无来时路。
t_en: Turning back suddenly, you realize, the path you came from is no longer there.

t_ch: 意义不明的金属元件，其上的星月纹样强调着它从属于晨昏之眼、并非凡物。
t_en: An enigmatic metal component, adorned with star-and-moon patterns that clearly signify its belonging to the Eye of Twilight. It is no ordinary object.

t_ch: 这些元件里面传出了嘀嗒声，该不会——虽然可能性不大，但还是希望它别炸。
t_en: A ticking sound emerges from within the components. Could it be...? Though unlikely, you still hope it doesn't explode.

t_ch: 艾格勒用这些装置记录着翁法罗斯的历史，可惜你不具备阅读它们的能力。
t_en: Aquila used these devices to record the history of Amphoreus, but unfortunately, you lack the ability to read them.

t_ch: 装满卷轴的书匣，从它散乱的现况能看出其主人逃亡时的惊慌。
t_en: A book case filled with scrolls. Its disordered state reveals the panic of its owner during their escape.

t_ch: 你粗略地翻看了几张卷轴。数额巨大的欠条、太过晦涩的专著，以及你本不该着眼的日记……
t_en: You skim through a few scrolls. Massive debt notes, overly obscure treatises, and a personal diary you probably shouldn't be reading...

t_ch: 为什么要在这种危机关头打开书匣？你知道有人会在临死前格式化电脑磁盘，或许出于同一个道理。
t_en: Why open the book case at such a critical moment? You know some people choose to format their computer disks before death, perhaps for the same reason.

t_ch: 昔日流淌着黄金流体的圣烛此时暗淡无光，颓然地熄灭在黄昏的暮色里。
t_en: The sacred candle, once flowing with golden fluid, now lies dim and extinguished, helplessly fading into the dusk of twilight.

t_ch: 或许是因为再创世在即，四溢的神性也随之开始重新凝聚。你伸手触碰它，却没有得到任何回应。
t_en: Perhaps it is because the Era Nova is imminent, and the overflowing divine energy has begun to coalesce once more. You reach out to touch it, but receive no response.

t_ch: 门廊顶部的巨型圆灯投射出如日光般的光辉，如此高大的门扉，你不禁猜想，这扇门原本是为谁而设？
t_en: The giant circular lamp atop the porch casts a radiance akin to sunlight. Faced with such towering doors, you can't help but wonder: For whom were these gates originally designed?

t_ch: 门扉顶部的巨眼闪烁着苍白的光芒，它正审视着每一位胆敢靠近的访客。
t_en: The giant eye atop the door flickers with a pale light, scrutinizing every visitor who dares to approach.

t_ch: 大门上方的光源发出柔和的蓝光，在黎明与黄昏的交替中，它见证了翁法罗斯的兴衰。而你的出现，只是它漫长凝视中的一个瞬间。
t_en: The light above the gate emits a soft blue glow, witnessing the rise and fall of Amphoreus between dawn and dusk. Your presence is but a fleeting moment in its long, unwavering gaze.

t_ch: 壁画以金线勾勒出天文仪器、星座图谱与天象变化，艾格勒对翁法罗斯的观测与掌控跃然壁上。你不禁思考，当百眼凝视星空时，它看到了什么？
t_en: The mural, outlined in gold, depicts astronomical instruments, constellation charts, and celestial phenomena. Aquila's observation and control of Amphoreus come alive on the wall. You can't help but wonder: What did the Hundred Eyes see when it gazed upon the stars?

t_ch: 壁画上描绘着工具、酒杯与令人费解的器具，这是泰坦的象征，亦或翁法罗斯历史的剪影？或许两者皆是。
t_en: The mural depicts tools, goblets, and perplexing implements, symbols of the Titans, or perhaps glimpses of Amphoreus' history? Perhaps both.

t_ch: 纹金壁画在深色背景里熠熠生辉，记录着过往的故事与荣光。千百年过去，唯有这些死物依然闪耀。
t_en: The gilded murals shimmer against the dark background, recording stories and glories of the past. Thousands of years have passed, yet only these lifeless objects continue to shine.

t_ch: 曾经高悬的巨物们，如今已悉数落入金池。这会不会也是彼时天空之民们的一种写照呢？
t_en: The once-suspended giants have now all fallen into the golden pool. Could this also be a reflection of the Skyfolk from that time?

t_ch: 滚烫的黄金血沸腾翻涌，即便站在此处，你也感到了扑面而来的热浪烘烤着你的面颊。
t_en: The boiling golden blood churns and surges. Even standing here, you feel the scorching heatwave baking against your cheeks.

t_ch: 黄金裔身体里流着这样的血液？你真为他们感到担心。
t_en: The descendants of Chrysos Heir have such blood flowing in their veins? You truly feel concerned for them.

t_ch: 沸腾的池水散发着令人窒息的热量，有时候，最闪耀的黄金并非容器中的流体，而是甘愿为世界牺牲的魂灵。
t_en: The boiling pool emits suffocating heat. Sometimes, the most radiant gold is not the fluid in the vessel, but the souls willing to sacrifice themselves for the world.

t_ch: 熔炉涡心发出令人畏惧的光亮，泰坦的血液在此沸腾。你小心翼翼地站在边缘，感受着它的热量拍打脸颊。也许这就是黄金裔的归宿——回归星火，再创新生。
t_en: The vortex at the heart of the furnace emits a fearsome glow, where the blood of Titans boils. You cautiously stand at the edge, feeling its heat beat against your cheeks. Perhaps this is the destiny of the Chrysos Heir, to return to the starlit flames and be reborn anew.

t_ch: 你低头凝视，思考着这一切的由来。你觉得有人在呼唤你的名字，但四下无人——或许只是炉心的高温令你产生的幻觉。
t_en: You lower your head and gaze, pondering the origin of all this. You feel as though someone is calling your name, but no one is around, perhaps it's just an illusion brought on by the intense heat of the furnace core.

t_ch: 矗立数千年的建筑仿佛突然间从这个世界抽离，只留下泛着幽蓝萤光的虚影。
t_en: The structures that have stood for thousands of years seem to have suddenly been pulled away from this world, leaving only faint, glowing blue shadows behind.

t_ch: 你凝神观察，虚影的末端正以缓慢却无可置疑的速度侵吞着大理石，横切面如蠕虫般涌动。
t_en: You focus intently, observing as the edges of the shadow slowly but unmistakably consume the marble, the cross-section writhing like worms.

t_ch: 你不知道这意味着什么，但显然不是一个好兆头。
t_en: You don't know what this means, but it's clearly not a good sign.

t_ch: 这是离刻法勒最近之处，你的整个视野几乎都被他遮天蔽日的神躯占据。
t_en: This is the closest point to Kephale. Your entire vision is almost entirely occupied by their towering, sky-obscuring divine form.

t_ch: 你可以清晰地看见岁月在泰坦身上凿磨出的痕迹，而它胸口流淌的金血，似乎比往日暗淡了些。
t_en: You can clearly see the marks left by time on the Titan's body, and the golden blood flowing from their chest seems dimmer than before.

t_ch: 你呼唤泰坦的名字，只得到一段漫长的沉默。你甚至没有听到回音。
t_en: You call out the Titan's name, but only receive silence as an answer. Not even an echo can be heard.

t_ch: 逐火的使命走到了尽头。刻法勒缄默，曾流向大地的金血也已枯竭干涸。
t_en: The Flame-Chase mission has come to an end. Kephale remains silent, and the golden blood that once flowed across the land has dried up.

t_ch: 你想起在这里宴饮的时光，金血沉浮，为池水镀上亮色。友人举杯，谈笑间觥筹交错。
t_en: You remember the feasts held here — the golden blood shimmering in the pool, friends raising their glasses, laughter ringing through the air.

t_ch: 现在这里一片寂静。满目疮痍，触目可见的只有回忆。
t_en: Now, only silence remains. Ruins stretch before you, and all that lingers are memories.

t_ch: 温和的水流已经冷却。冰凉的池水颤动音管，水漫琴的音色似乎比平日更加凄清悠扬。
t_en: The once-warm waters have cooled. The trembling pipes hum as the cold water passes through them, making the sound of water lyre even more wistful than usual.

t_ch: 你感到寒意沿着双足攀上身体，唤起一股糅杂着悲凉与落寞的复杂情绪。
t_en: A chill creeps up from your feet, wrapping around your body, and stirring a mix of sorrow and solitude.

t_ch: 或许不该在这里停留太久……
t_en: Maybe you shouldn't stay here for too long...

t_ch: 拱卫圣城步道的塔楼曾是瞭望敌情的哨站，现在仿佛被剥离，空余盘踞在石峦上的虚影。
t_en: The towers that once guarded the holy city's pathways stood as watchposts against approaching threats. Now, they seem detached from time, mere shadows lingering on the stones.

t_ch: 难道这就是所谓的再创世，而你脚下的奥赫玛也会变成一片虚空？
t_en: Is this what Era Nova truly means? Will the Okhema beneath your feet also fade into nothingness?

t_ch: 不知道其中的卫兵如何了…你有些关心，毕竟那恐怕也是你即将面临的命运。
t_en: What happened to the guards stationed here? You wonder for a moment—perhaps because their fate might soon be yours.

t_ch: 驾驭大地兽向世界末日的最后一个黄昏奔去，留给翁法罗斯一个背影、一段传说，是个足够浪漫的好主意。
t_en: Riding a dromas into the last sunset of the world, leaving Amphoreus with only a legend and a silhouette from behind — sounds romantic enough.

t_ch: 可惜大地兽已经被巡街的队伍征用，不会再回应你的呼唤。
t_en: Too bad the dromas has already been requisitioned by the patrols. It won't answer you again.

t_ch: 浪漫和理智真的是天生一对吗？还是说，一对注定会煎熬到双双殉情的苦主？
t_en: Are romance and reason really a perfect pair? Or are they just doomed lovers, destined to burn out together?

t_ch: 木马安安静静地伫立在这里。或许，你错过了「早些时候」与它的交流，以及一个「约定」。
t_en: The wooden horse stands there, silent. Maybe you missed your chance to speak to it "earlier"... and the "promise" that came with it.

t_ch: 呵，来的正是时候，借着你提供的记忆做指引，我从门径的尽头攫来了一批新奇玩意，全都堆在里面了，商店大更新！
t_en: Ha! Just in time. With the memories you've given me as my guide, I grabbed a fresh batch of curiosities from the end of the Passage. They're all in there and the shop's got a major restock!

t_ch: ——当然，如果你想得到它们，你得用更多「记忆」来拿。我猜你明白这规矩。
t_en: Of course, if you want them, you'll need to pay with more memories. You know the deal.

t_ch: （拿出记忆残晶）
t_en: (Take out the Memory Crystal Shard)

t_ch: 还会有其他商品吗？
t_en: Will there be more items later?

t_ch: 没了没了，不都说是门径的尽头了嘛——除非这世界重新来过，不然我也不知道上哪去给你找更多稀奇古怪的东西。
t_en: Nope, that's it. Didn't I just say it was the end of the Passage? Unless the world resets itself, I've got nowhere else to scavenge more weird and wonderful things for you.

t_ch: 再说，第二批了，还没买够？你的「记忆」可真多啊。
t_en: Besides, this is already the second batch. Still not enough for you? You must have a ton of memories to spare.

t_ch: 想要和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to have a chat with you.

t_ch: 哼，能和神驹对话可是种荣耀。要不是背井离乡，我本该向你收取报酬的…算了，你说吧。
t_en: Hmph. Consider yourself lucky — talking to a divine foal is an honor. If I hadn't left my hometown, I'd charge you for this... Forget it. Just speak your mind.

t_ch: 你从哪进的货？
t_en: Where do you get your stock from?

t_ch: 命运之初，门径之末，塔兰顿家的屋顶上，瑟西斯常去的餐馆里——
t_en: The dawn of fate, the end of Passage, the rooftops of Talanton's estate, and Cerces' favorite restaurant...

t_ch: 当然，以上纯属胡诌，换言之就是「保密」。别问啦，只要知道都是珍品就行。
t_en: Obviously, I made all that up. In other words — classified information. Don't ask. Just know they're all treasures.

t_ch: 再创世就要来了，你……
t_en: Era Nova is coming, you...

t_ch: 再创世？啊，那个预言！怪不得刚刚有怪物在，原来是变天了啊。我还让一个奥赫玛人在神驹里面躲了躲呢。
t_en: Era Nova? Oh, that prophecy! No wonder the monsters were acting up, I guess we're in for a ride. I even let an Okheman hide inside the divine foal for a bit.

t_ch: 嗯，好事，这代表我又能见到其他神驹们了。要是遇上那几个消极怠工的，我非得把它们的脑袋漆成绿的不可。
t_en: Eh, works for me. This means I get to see the other divine foals again. And if I run into those slackers, I swear I'm painting their heads green.

t_ch: 我下次再来。
t_en: I'll come back next time.

t_ch: 去吧，记得多准备些「记忆」！
t_en: Go ahead. Just don't forget to bring more memories!

t_ch: 哼，超级神秘商品——法吉娜曾经摆在寝宫内的海螺！
t_en: Hmph, the ultra-mysterious goods: Phagousa's conch! Straight from their royal chambers!

t_ch: 它的信徒们相信，只要你够虔诚，就能听见海螺壳对你诉说自然的美好。
t_en: Their followers believed that if you were devout enough, the conch shell would whisper the beauty of nature to you.

t_ch: 他们听没听见我不知道，但这海螺是真的能说。戳一下那根刺，听听看吧。
t_en: I don't know if they actually hear anything, but the conch does talk. Give that little spike a poke and listen for yourself.

t_ch: ——传来了自然的声音。
t_en: The sound of nature echoed forth.

t_ch: 有意思吧？还没完呢，这东西和神驹有点像，把独特的记忆喂给它，它能「回忆」起更多音色。至于那些记忆去哪找…我进的货里就有，你可以抽空挑挑。
t_en: Intriguing, huh? But it's not over yet. This thing is a bit like a divine foal — feed it unique memories, and it will recall more tones. As for where to find those memories... I've got some in stock. Take your time browsing.

t_ch: 总之，现在它是你的了，拿去玩吧。
t_en: Anyway, it's yours now. Have fun with it.

t_ch: 等回到列车上，就把这东西<color=#dbc291ff>摆到留声机旁边</color>好了。
t_en: Once you're back on the Express, set this thing <color=#dbc291ff>right next to the phonograph</color>.

t_ch: 你将其记录…愿西风为人们带去宁静。
t_en: You record it... May the west wind bring peace to all.

t_ch: 记忆化作音色，流淌在海螺的「言语」中——你可以聆听其中之一，或是许多。
t_en: Memories take shape as tones, flowing through the conch's "words." You may listen to one... or many.

t_ch: 聆听「燃薪」。
t_en: Listen to "Kindling."

t_ch: 不再聆听「燃薪」。
t_en: Stop listening to "Kindling."

t_ch: 聆听「冬」。
t_en: Listen to "Winter."

t_ch: 不再聆听「冬」。
t_en: Stop listening to "Winter."

t_ch: 聆听「雨中」。
t_en: Listen to "In the Rain."

t_ch: 不再聆听「雨中」。
t_en: Stop listening to "In the Rain."

t_ch: 聆听「隆隆」。
t_en: Listen to "Rumble."

t_ch: 不再聆听「隆隆」。
t_en: Stop listening to "Rumble."

t_ch: 先这样吧。
t_en: That's it for now.

t_ch: 虹光洒落的地方…你的目光是不是也落在那里？
t_en: Where the rainbow falls... is your gaze there as well?

t_ch: 望着涡心闪烁的群星，你想起那位正支撑着天穹的医者。
t_en: Looking at the stars shining in the center of the vortex, you think of the healer who is currently supporting the sky dome.

t_ch: 她用精湛的医术疗愈伤口，用温柔的微笑弥合疮痕。她形单影只，肩负起重返天空的使命…即便神谕允诺的祝福只不过是诅咒的回音。
t_en: She heals wounds with her superb medical skills and mends scars with her gentle smile. She travels alone, shouldering the mission of returning to the sky... even though the blessings promised by the prophecy are but echoes of a curse.

t_ch: 无论在哪个世界，总有人秉持着守护的志愿，憧憬即将到来的明天。
t_en: No matter the world, there are always those who uphold a will to protect others, dreaming of the tomorrow that's yet to come.

t_ch: 她总是独自承受……
t_en: She always bears the burden alone...

t_ch: 赛飞儿…不知道她还好吗？
t_en: Cipher... I wonder if she's okay?

t_ch: 为保护刻法勒的火种，赛飞儿只身拖延着盗火行者。但那黑衣剑士最终还是回到了奥赫玛，这难道意味着……
t_en: To protect the Coreflame of Kephale, Cipher delayed the Flame Reaver alone. But that black-robed swordmaster eventually returned to Okhema. Does this mean...

t_ch: 那位步伐轻巧的盗贼有的是脱身的手段，应该不会为了这种事搭上性命吧？可是，她真的不会吗？若如预言所说，最终只有一人得以觐见奇迹……
t_en: That light-footed thief has many ways of escaping. She wouldn't risk her life over something like this, right...? Would she really? But... if the prophecy is true... then only one person will ultimately witness the miracle...

t_ch: 「小灰子啊，你不会真信了这唬人的玩意吧？」你仿佛听见一声熟悉的讥笑在耳边响起。但待你凝神细听，却只听见浪声交叠。
t_en: "Gray Mystery, you don't really believe in this bluff, did you?" You seem to hear a familiar mocking laugh in your ear. But when you focus and listen closely, you hear nothing but the sound of waves intertwining.

t_ch: 没错…即便要付出一切，也应当把命运掌握在自己手里。
t_en: That's right... even if it costs everything, we should take control of our own fate.

t_ch: 安静下来了……
t_en: It's quiet now...

t_ch: 你回想起车厢跨过云层被长枪洞穿的那一刻，那是冒险的伊始。在那之后，你目睹了燃尽血火的抗争，涉过月色下的花海…直到如今，主宰翁法罗斯命运的星辰渐次亮起。
t_en: You recall the moment the Express Car crossed the clouds and was pierced by the long spear. That was the beginning of the adventure. After that, you witnessed the strife that burnt out humanity's blood and walked through the sea of flowers under the moonlight... until now, when the stars that govern Amphoreus' fate are slowly lighting up.

t_ch: 在那星光之下，涡流仿佛被一股宁静而浩大的力量牵引，沉默地吞吐着水面的波澜。它未曾回应你的目光，更从未被你的思绪打动。
t_en: Under that starlight, the vortex seems to be quietly pulled by a vast and tranquil force, silently swallowing the waves on the water's surface. It never responds to your gaze, nor is it ever moved by your thoughts.

t_ch: 是不是每场旅途都会始于喧嚣，归于宁静？你有预感，故事并未就此结束。而新的故事会如何开启，将由你来见证。
t_en: Is every journey destined to begin in noise and end in tranquility? You have a premonition that the story isn't over yet, and you will be the one to witness how a new story begins.

t_ch: 这就是无名客的使命，不是吗？
t_en: That is the mission of the Nameless, isn't it?

t_ch: 「岁月」泰坦的神像，没有面孔，和你在命运重渊见过的颇为相似。
t_en: A faceless statue of the Time Titan stands here, much like the one you encountered in the Abyss of Fate.

t_ch: 它静静地注视着村落的一切，就像一棵历经风霜的树。
t_en: It stands in silent vigil over the village, like an ancient tree that has weathered countless storms.

t_ch: 或许这座小村庄确实有「岁月」的庇护，不然它怎么会像是完全不受岁月侵蚀一样？
t_en: Maybe this tiny village really is under the protection of "Time." How else could it remain so perfectly preserved against the ravages of time?

t_ch: 一阵恍惚涌上心头，你仿佛听见了来自往昔的声音。
t_en: A sudden dizziness washes over you. For a moment, you think you hear echoes from the distant past.

t_ch: 你胡说，一个下午怎么可能钓到四十条鱼？
t_en: You're making that up! How could anyone catch forty fish in just one afternoon?

t_ch: 我没有胡说！
t_en: I'm not!

t_ch: 那鱼呢？
t_en: Then where are these fish now?

t_ch: 我、我全放了！
t_en: I—I set them all free!

t_ch: 那孩子，还是嚷嚷着要去外面看看…可万一遇上什么麻烦，我们在家里又帮不上忙，真放心不下啊。
t_en: The kid is still talking about going out to see the world... but I'm really worried. What if something happens to him out there, and we're too far away to do anything?

t_ch: 他已经长大了，咱们做父母的也得学着放手，总不能事事都按照自己的想法来。
t_en: Hey, he's not a child anymore. We parents have to learn to let go. We can't control everything in his life.

t_ch: 出去闯荡一番挺好的。哪怕什么都学不到，也比在家种麦子更能涨见识。
t_en: Heading out into the world is not a bad idea. Even if you don't learn a thing, it still beats staying home growing wheat.

t_ch: …说的也是，那我去往他的行囊里多塞点面包。
t_en: ...Fair point. I'll toss a few extra loaves into his pack before he goes.

t_ch: 破碎的战旗、折断的箭矢…在久远的过去，冥河之下似乎曾发生过激烈的战斗。
t_en: Tattered war banners and broken arrows... It seems a fierce battle took place beneath the River of Souls long ago.

t_ch: 战争，战争从不改变。
t_en: War... war never changes.

t_ch: 等披索那小子再大一点，我就把这家传的宝弓交给他，教他射箭的本领。
t_en: Once Piso's a little older, I'll pass down the family's prized bow and teach him how to shoot properly.

t_ch: 以后，他会成为村里的新猎户。我也能好好歇歇，每天安心钓鱼、喝茶、享清福了。
t_en: One day, he'll be the village's new hunter. And I'll finally get to kick back — fishing, tea-sipping, living the good retired life.

t_ch: 哈哈，我就没那么省心了。我要是退休，村里一时半会可找不出能接班的老师。
t_en: Ha! I'm not as lucky as you are. If I retired, it'd be a while before this village found another teacher who could fill my shoes.

t_ch: 或许等白厄他学成归来之后，我也该去外面的世界走上一圈。
t_en: Maybe once Phainon returns, trained and ready, I'll take a trip myself and see what's out there in the world.

t_ch: 你看到有些长矛呈现出倾斜的角度，它们似乎从遥远的地方被人投掷到此。
t_en: You notice some spears lodged at an angle, as if they had been thrown here from afar.

t_ch: 丹恒曾经说过「击云从不离身」——现在想来，「击云」离身的次数还是挺多的。
t_en: Dan Heng once said, "Cloud-Piercer never leaves my side" — but in hindsight, it sure has left his side plenty of times.

t_ch: 桌上摆着水果蛋糕、水果馅饼，还有水果…看来庭院中的那位少女有着一双巧手，以及用来装甜食的第二个胃。
t_en: There's fruit cake, fruit pie, and... more fruit on the table... Looks like that girl in the courtyard's got magic hands and a second stomach just for sweets.

t_ch: 丰盛的食物静静陈列，仿佛在等待一场盛宴——或许将来的某天，你能与伙伴们在此齐聚、共同分享。
t_en: A feast laid out in silence, as if waiting for the party to start. Maybe one day, you and your companions will gather here and dig in together.

t_ch: 地毯、靠垫、柔软的枕头、解馋的零食…毫无疑问，这是一处精心布置的冥想点。
t_en: Cushions, rugs, soft pillows, and a stash of snacks... Yep, this is someone's carefully curated meditation corner.

t_ch: 据说命运泰坦的祭司们会长时间闭目打坐，以求进入某种通灵状态…不排除有人会在途中睡着。
t_en: Word is, the Fate Titans' priests meditate for hours, seeking spiritual clarity... though let's be real, some probably just doze off.

t_ch: 通往建筑内部的木门，似乎上了锁。你试着推了一下，只能听见合页发出吱嘎的抗议声。
t_en: The wooden door leading inside the building seems locked. You give it a push, and all you get is a cranky squeak from the hinges.

t_ch: 面朝大海，春暖花开…旁边还摆着不知谁人的钓竿。
t_en: Facing the sea, the flowers bloom in spring... and someone's fishing rod lies waiting nearby.

t_ch: 都说海钓的难度很高，但也不是非得钓到鱼才算是成功。
t_en: They say sea fishing's tough, but hey, landing a catch isn't the only way to win.

t_ch: 有时，洋流会送来意想不到的惊喜——比如一个漂流瓶，又或是一条自己蹦上岸的鱼。
t_en: Sometimes the currents bring surprises, like a drift bottle or a fish that jumps right onto shore.

t_ch: 装有成袋面粉的板车，似乎在等待船只将它们运走。
t_en: A cart stacked with sacks of flour, ready to be shipped out by boat.

t_ch: 麦粒与面粉是哀丽秘榭的主要物产，凝结着村民们的汗水与心血。
t_en: Grain and flour are key exports from Aedes Elysiae, each sack packed with the villagers' sweat and pride.

t_ch: 传说在翁法罗斯的神话故事中，悬锋城的某位英雄从出生起就被母亲倒提着浸入冥河之中，冥河之水被认为具有赋予人不死之身的神奇力量。
t_en: According to the myths of Amphoreus, a certain hero of Castrum Kremnos was dipped upside down into the River of Souls at birth by his mother, believing its waters would grant him invulnerability.

t_ch: 然而，由于这位母亲一直捏着英雄的脚踝，导致脚踝部位没有被冥河水浸泡到，从而留下了唯一「弱点」。
t_en: However, because the mother held him by his ankle, that spot remained untouched by the river, leaving a single weak point.

t_ch: 这冥河水真像故事中描绘的那么神奇？实践是检验真理的唯一标准，这里没有欧洛尼斯的奇迹保护你，你决定——
t_en: Is this river truly as miraculous as the legends say? There's only one way to find out — experimentation. After all, truth is tested through practice, and here, no miracle of Oronyx will protect you. You decide to —

t_ch: 实践又不一定要在自己身上嘛…
t_en: Wait a second... who says the experiment has to be on yourself?

t_ch: 装上一大桶，带回浴场大家用！
t_en: Scoop up a whole bucket and bring it back to the baths for everyone to try!

t_ch: 可惜冥河水并非是真正的水，它只是一种特殊的现象，是带不走的。
t_en: Unfortunately, the River of Souls isn't actual water. It's a phenomenon, something you cannot take with you.

t_ch: 不过，你可以试试回浴场号召那些喜欢的泡澡的朋友跟你一起过来。
t_en: But you could try rallying your bath-loving friends to come here and test it out with you.

t_ch: 看我把黑潮造物给推进冥河里。
t_en: See how I shove Black Tide Creatures into the River of Souls.

t_ch: 这些怪物真是倒了大霉了，成天自己吓自己，终于是被你吓到了。
t_en: Those monsters are seriously unlucky — scaring themselves all day long, only to end up getting scared by you instead.

t_ch: 噗通——
t_en: Plop —

t_ch: 「请问你掉的是这个金色的怪物，还是这个银色的怪物？」
t_en: "Tell me, did you drop this golden monster, or this silver one?"

t_ch: 我没有掉怪物。
t_en: I didn't drop any monsters.

t_ch: 「不好意思，是我来的不是时候。」
t_en: "Oh, my apologies. Seems I came at the wrong time."

t_ch: 我掉的是金色的怪物。
t_en: I dropped the golden monster.

t_ch: 「不诚实的孩子，如果我能惩罚你就惩罚你了。」
t_en: "Dishonest child. If only I could punish you..."

t_ch: 我掉的是普通的怪物。
t_en: I dropped a regular monster.

t_ch: 「诚实的孩子，如果我有怪物就给你发怪物了。」
t_en: "What an honest child. If I had a monster to give, I would offer it to you."

t_ch: 我什么都没看到，现编是吧？
t_en: I didn't see anything. You're just making this up, aren't you?

t_ch: 哎，你出戏太快。
t_en: Wow, way to kill the immersion.

t_ch: 总之，冥河对黑潮怪物影响不大，再说了，这下面水太深了，你懂的。
t_en: Anyways, the River of Souls doesn't really do much to those monsters. And let's be real — this water runs deep.

t_ch: 总之先把球棒扎进去试试？
t_en: Might as well poke it with the bat and see what happens?

t_ch: 恭喜你得到了「不死的球棒」，它的弱点在你握住它的地方。
t_en: Congrats! You've obtained the Undying Bat! Its weak point is wherever you're holding it.

t_ch: 感觉也没什么可试的了。
t_en: Welp, that about covers all the experiments.

t_ch: 不如自己亲自试试。
t_en: Or... I could try it myself.

t_ch: 为了你的大部分身体完整，为了不要「一不小心」就彻底掉进去——你的尝试先从「泡脚」开始。
t_en: For the sake of keeping most of your body intact and avoiding "a whoops," full submersion situation — you decide to start with just your feet.
